items.title: Chain-of-Thought Degrades Visual Spatial Reasoning Capabilities of Multimodal LLMs
items.abstract: This paper demonstrates that using Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting significantly degrades the visual spatial reasoning performance of Multimodal Large Language Models (MRMs) across numerous benchmarks. The authors reveal this degradation is linked to shortcut learning, where models hallucinate visual details from textual priors even without an image present. This challenges the reliance on text-only CoT for spatial tasks, advocating for vision-centric reasoning methods instead.
items.figure_caption: (Left) CoT vs Non-CoT performance of open-source MRMs. (Right) Bar chart showing the average accuracy of various families of MLMs over 13 benchmark datasets. For each model, the left bar shows the accuracy achieved by CoT prompting, and the right bar shows for base prompt (non-CoT). We observe CoT prompting drops performance over a wide range of backbones and model scales, including Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking Bai et al. ( 2025a ) , a model with explicitly enhanced spatial perception.

items.title: Do Vision-Language Models Truly Perform Vision Reasoning? A Rigorous Study of the Modality Gap
items.abstract: This paper rigorously investigates whether Vision-Language Models (VLMs) perform genuine vision-grounded reasoning or rely on their textual components. The core method introduces **CrossMath**, a novel benchmark where problems are presented in text-only, image-only, and combined formats with strictly identical information across modalities. The contribution is the demonstration of a **substantial performance gap**, showing VLMs significantly excel at text-only reasoning compared to image-based reasoning, suggesting a weakness in true vision grounding.

items.title: Dual-Modal Lung Cancer AI: Interpretable Radiology and Microscopy with Clinical Risk Integration
items.abstract: This paper introduces a dual-modal AI framework that fuses features extracted from lung CT scans and H\&E histopathology, augmented with clinical data, for lung cancer diagnosis and subtype classification. The core method uses CNNs for feature extraction and a weighted decision-level fusion mechanism to improve diagnostic robustness. The contribution lies in creating a more comprehensive and interpretable AI system by integrating radiology and microscopy for better lung cancer characterization.
items.figure_caption: Example chest CT images demonstrating a suspected lung tumor region across axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. CT imaging is commonly used for lung cancer screening and detection.

items.title: MARCH: Multi-Agent Radiology Clinical Hierarchy for CT Report Generation
items.abstract: MARCH is a multi-agent framework that mimics the hierarchical structure of radiology departments to improve CT report generation. It assigns specialized roles—Resident for drafting, Fellows for retrieval-augmented revision, and an Attending for orchestrating consensus discourse—to iteratively verify findings and reduce clinical hallucinations. This approach significantly enhances both the clinical fidelity and linguistic accuracy of the generated reports compared to monolithic models.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the MARCH framework. It consists of three main stages: 1) Initial Report Drafting, 2) Retrieval-Augmented Report Revision, and 3) Consensus-Driven Finalization.

items.title: DINOv3 Beats Specialized Detectors: A Simple Foundation Model Baseline for Image Forensics
items.abstract: This paper introduces DINOv3 adapted with LoRA and a lightweight decoder as a simple yet powerful foundation model baseline for image forensics, specifically for manipulation localization. The core method leverages the frozen DINOv3 backbone's rich pre-trained representations, fine-tuned efficiently via LoRA, which consistently outperforms full fine-tuning. This approach significantly surpasses specialized detectors, achieving substantial F1 score improvements across multiple benchmarks with far fewer trainable parameters.
items.figure_caption: Overview of our framework. A frozen DINOv3 ViT backbone with LoRA injected on QKV projections produces dense patch tokens, which are reshaped into a 2D feature map and decoded by a lightweight convolutional segmentation head into a pixel-level manipulation mask.

items.title: Elucidating the SNR-t Bias of Diffusion Probabilistic Models
items.abstract: This paper identifies and analyzes the **SNR-t bias** in Diffusion Probabilistic Models, where the Signal-to-Noise Ratio of the denoised sample misaligns with its timestep during inference, degrading generation quality. The authors provide theoretical and empirical evidence for this issue. They propose a simple, effective **differential correction method** that leverages the frequency-dependent nature of the denoising process to mitigate this bias.

items.title: IA-CLAHE: Image-Adaptive Clip Limit Estimation for CLAHE
items.abstract: This paper introduces **IA-CLAHE**, an adaptive method for Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE). It addresses the issue of fixed clip limits causing over-enhancement by adaptively estimating tile-wise clip limits directly from the input image. This is achieved by training a lightweight estimator using a differentiable version of CLAHE, allowing for end-to-end optimization without needing pre-searched ground-truth limits.
items.figure_caption: Comparison between conventional CLAHE and the proposed IA-CLAHE. (a) Conventional CLAHE applies a single global clip limit to all tiles, visualized as the dashed line in the middle-left histogram. This fixed parameter often causes over-enhancement in already bright regions. (b) IA-CLAHE predicts tile-wise clip limits (orange lines in the middle-right histogram) using a lightweight estimator and applies them through a differentiable CLAHE module. This adaptive strategy enhances details in dark regions while preserving well-exposed areas.

items.title: Information Router for Mitigating Modality Dominance in Vision-Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces the **Multi-modal Information Router ($\text{MoIR}$)** to combat modality dominance in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by addressing information disparity *before* fusion. $\text{MoIR}$ explicitly identifies tokens from weaker modalities and routes complementary, informative signals from stronger modalities to them. This information-level routing mechanism ensures a more balanced contribution from both vision and language inputs, leading to more robust predictions than attention-based methods alone.

items.title: Mind's Eye: A Benchmark of Visual Abstraction, Transformation and Composition for Multimodal LLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces "Mind's Eye," a novel benchmark designed to assess the visual cognitive and visuospatial reasoning capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) using eight tasks categorized by Abstraction, Relation, and Transformation. The benchmark reveals that top MLLMs significantly underperform humans (below 50% vs. 80% accuracy), highlighting critical limitations in their visual attention, internal perceptual manipulation, and abstraction of visual concepts.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the eight tasks in the proposed Mind’s Eye benchmark : Each panel shows an example image-question pair of the benchmark

items.title: Ranking XAI Methods for Head and Neck Cancer Outcome Prediction
items.abstract: This paper comprehensively evaluates and ranks 13 Explainable AI (XAI) methods for predicting Head and Neck Cancer outcomes using PET/CT data, addressing the critical clinical barrier of AI interpretability. The evaluation uses 24 metrics across faithfulness, robustness, complexity, and plausibility, finding significant performance variations among methods. Integrated Gradients (IG) and DeepLIFT (DL) consistently ranked highly across key aspects, highlighting the necessity of thorough XAI assessment.

items.title: Where Do Vision-Language Models Fail? World Scale Analysis for Image Geolocalization
items.abstract: This paper systematically evaluates state-of-the-art Vision-Language Models (VLMs) for zero-shot, prompt-based country-level image geolocalization using only ground-view imagery. The core contribution is a world-scale analysis revealing significant performance variation across models. This highlights the potential of VLMs for coarse geographic inference while exposing their current limitations in capturing fine-grained spatial details.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the evaluation pipeline. A ground-level image and a structured prompt are passed to each of the three VLM families ( ≤ \( \leq \) 8B parameters), which output Top-5 country predictions. Performance is assessed via Top-1/Top-5 accuracy and a multi-dimensional evaluation framework comprising Environmental Stratification (urban/rural and six biome categories), Error Structure Analysis (neighbor hop distance), and Geographic Error Reasonableness (GER) score, the latter two applied only to incorrect predictions.

items.title: Enhancing Hazy Wildlife Imagery: AnimalHaze3k and IncepDehazeGan
items.abstract: This paper introduces **AnimalHaze3k**, a synthetic dataset of 3,477 hazy wildlife images created using a physics-based pipeline. The authors propose **IncepDehazeGan**, a novel GAN architecture utilizing inception blocks and residual connections, which achieves state-of-the-art dehazing performance. This method significantly improves downstream animal detection tasks, offering ecologists robust tools for wildlife monitoring.

items.title: FineCog-Nav: Integrating Fine-grained Cognitive Modules for Zero-shot Multimodal UAV Navigation
items.abstract: FineCog-Nav introduces a novel framework for zero-shot UAV navigation by integrating distinct, fine-grained cognitive modules (e.g., perception, memory, reasoning) inspired by human cognition. Each module, driven by a moderate foundation model with specific prompting, communicates via structured protocols to collaboratively execute complex, multi-step instructions. This approach enhances interpretability and performance over existing methods, supported by the creation of the AerialVLN-Fine benchmark for fine-grained evaluation.
items.figure_caption: We propose FineCog-Nav, a framework designed to simulate diverse human cognitive functions.

items.title: SWNet: A Cross-Spectral Network for Camouflaged Weed Detection
items.abstract: SWNet is a cross-spectral network designed to detect camouflaged weeds by fusing Visible and Near-Infrared (NIR) imagery. It employs a Pyramid Vision Transformer backbone and a Bimodal Gated Fusion Module to dynamically integrate spectral information, exploiting physiological differences invisible in the visible spectrum. This approach significantly improves discrimination accuracy for homochromatic weeds, further enhanced by an Edge-Aware Refinement module for sharper boundaries.
items.figure_caption: The overall architecture of the proposed SWNet.

items.description: FinceptTerminal is a modern finance application offering advanced market analytics, investment research, and economic data tools, designed for interactive exploration and data-driven decision-making in a user-friendly environment.
items.summary: FinceptTerminal is a modern finance application providing advanced market analytics, investment research, and economic data tools for interactive exploration and data-driven decision-making.

items.description: AI You Control: Choose your models. Own your data. Eliminate vendor lock-in.
items.summary: Thunderbolt is an AI platform enabling users to control their models, own their data, and avoid vendor lock-in by offering model choice.

items.description: Enterprise Architecture Governance & Vendor Procurement Toolkit
items.summary: Arc-Kit is an enterprise architecture governance and vendor procurement toolkit providing structured processes and resources for managing architectural standards and acquiring necessary technologies.

items.description: A lightweight, powerful framework for multi-agent workflows
items.summary: The `openai-agents-python` repository provides a lightweight, powerful Python framework designed to facilitate the creation and management of complex, multi-agent workflows powered by OpenAI models.

The `t3code` repository provides a comprehensive, opinionated starter kit for building full-stack web applications using the T3 Stack (Next.js, TypeScript, tRPC, Tailwind CSS) with integrated authentication and database setup.

items.description: A community-supported supercharged document management system: scan, index and archive all your documents
items.summary: Paperless-ngx is a community-supported document management system that allows users to scan, index, and archive all their documents efficiently.

items.description: π RuView: WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
items.summary: RuView is a WiFi-based system that transforms commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection without using any video.

items.description: The GEP-Powered Self-Evolution Engine for AI Agents. Genome Evolution Protocol. | evomap.ai
items.summary: EvoMap/evolver is a GEP-powered self-evolution engine designed for AI agents, utilizing a Genome Evolution Protocol to drive continuous improvement and adaptation.

items.description: AI that sees your screen, listens to your conversations and tells you what to do
items.summary: BasedHardware/omi is an AI system designed to actively observe a user's screen and listen to conversations to provide real-time guidance and instructions.

items.description: Turn Claude Code into a full game dev studio — 49 AI agents, 72 workflow skills, and a complete coordination system mirroring real studio hierarchy.
items.summary: This repository transforms Claude AI into a comprehensive game development studio, featuring 49 specialized AI agents, 72 workflow skills, and a complete coordination system mirroring real industry hierarchy.

items.title: Airline worker arrested after sharing photos of bomb damage in WhatsApp group
items.summary: An airline worker was arrested in Dubai after sharing photos of bomb damage in a private WhatsApp group.

items.title: Uber’s Anthropic AI push hits a wall
items.summary: Uber's efforts to integrate Anthropic's AI technology have reportedly stalled due to internal disagreements and challenges in aligning the technology with the company's needs.

items.title: CEOs admit AI had no impact on employment or productivity
items.summary: A recent study reveals that thousands of CEOs report that artificial intelligence has had no measurable impact on either their company's employment levels or overall productivity.

items.title: Swiss AI Initiative (2023)
items.summary: The Swiss AI Initiative 2023 is a national effort focused on advancing artificial intelligence research, development, and application within Switzerland.

hacker_news.summary: The Swiss AI Initiative 2023 is a national effort focused on advancing artificial intelligence research, development, and application within Switzerland.

github_trending.description: AI You Control: Choose your models. Own your data. Eliminate vendor lock-in.
github_trending.summary: Thunderbolt is an AI platform enabling users to control their models, own their data, and avoid vendor lock-in by offering model choice.

github_trending.description: Enterprise Architecture Governance & Vendor Procurement Toolkit
github_trending.summary: Arc-Kit is an enterprise architecture governance and vendor procurement toolkit providing structured processes and resources for managing architectural standards and acquiring necessary technologies.

github_trending.description: A lightweight, powerful framework for multi-agent workflows
github_trending.summary: The `openai-agents-python` repository provides a lightweight, powerful Python framework designed to facilitate the creation and management of complex, multi-agent workflows powered by OpenAI models.

The `t3code` repository provides a comprehensive, opinionated starter kit for building full-stack web applications using the T3 Stack (Next.js, TypeScript, tRPC, Tailwind CSS) with integrated authentication and database setup.

github_trending.description: A community-supported supercharged document management system: scan, index and archive all your documents
github_trending.summary: Paperless-ngx is a community-supported document management system that allows users to scan, index, and archive all their documents efficiently.

github_trending.description: π RuView: WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
github_trending.summary: RuView is a WiFi-based system that transforms commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection without using any video.

github_trending.description: The GEP-Powered Self-Evolution Engine for AI Agents. Genome Evolution Protocol. | evomap.ai
github_trending.summary: EvoMap/evolver is a GEP-powered self-evolution engine designed for AI agents, utilizing a Genome Evolution Protocol to drive continuous improvement and adaptation.

github_trending.description: AI that sees your screen, listens to your conversations and tells you what to do
github_trending.summary: BasedHardware/omi is an AI system designed to actively observe a user's screen and listen to conversations to provide real-time guidance and instructions.
